figures.caption: Figure 2: LayerScale speedup over vLLM and SGLang on median per-turn latency, with no caching of any kind in play. The multiplier roughly doubles as the workflow deepens from 6 to 35 turns, reflecting the O(∆t) versus O(nt) per-turn cost difference between delta-only processing and full re-prefill.

Figure 3: Cumulative wall time over the 35-turn novel coding workflow (no response-cache hits). LayerScale finishes in roughly half the wall time, and the gap widens with every turn: it processes only the per-turn delta while vLLM and SGLang re-prefill the full and growing conversation on each turn. The separation is the compounding cost of redundant prefix recomputation.

Figure 1: Median per-turn latency on novel, fully-generated tool-call workloads (no response-cache hits). LayerScale is 2.1× faster than vLLM on the 6-turn agentic workflow and 4.2× faster on the median turn of the 35-turn deep-coding workflow; the gap widens as the reusable conversation prefix deepens.
